steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense form
Changed to command form by adding damma on first letter and sukun at the end. The middle weak letter و is dropped in this form.

steps.m: caller, one who addresses (in nominative case)
steps.n: Adding damma to show it's the subject
Added the vocative particle يَٰٓ to show this is calling out to someone
The ها makes it clear we're talking to multiple people

steps.m: to reject, disbelieve, or cover up truth

steps.n: Changes the verb into a noun describing someone who does this action
The masculine sound plural is formed by adding ون to indicate more than two disbelievers
Adding ال makes it refer to specific disbelievers. This word is being directly addressed in the ayah with يٰٓأَيُّهَا (O you...)
This is a negative particle that tells us something is not happening or should not happen
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation
Changed alif to long alif (آ) to show emphasis in pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the past tense form
Added أَ at start to show 'I' do the action, with sukun and damma vowels to show it's happening now. This verb is negated by لَآ before it, meaning 'I do not worship'
This is a versatile word that can mean 'what' as a question or 'that which' as a relative pronoun
Added the fatha mark over م to show the correct pronunciation. In this ayah, مَا is being used to ask about what is being worshipped, connecting to the verb أَعْبُدُ (I worship).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Added ت at start to show 'you' are doing it, changed vowels to show it's happening now
Added ون at the end to show multiple people are doing the action
Added the fatha vowel mark on ل and lengthened the alif to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
The و at the start means 'and', connecting this phrase to what came before it. This word negates عٰبِدُونَ (worshippers) in the ayah.
This is the basic form of the pronoun used when talking to multiple people
Adding م at the end makes it clear we're talking to multiple people. This pronoun is the subject (doer) of the word عٰبِدُونَ (worshippers) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he worshipped' to 'worshippers' by adding ون to make it plural. The middle vowel changed to show it's a noun (doer of action) instead of a verb. Matches with أَنتُمْ (you all)
This is a basic particle word that doesn't come from a root. In this ayah, it refers to 'what' as in 'what I worship'.
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation
Extended the alif for emphasis. This word is the object of the verb أَعْبُدُ (I worship) in this ayah, showing what is being worshipped.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the past tense verb
Added أَ at beginning to show 'I' and changed vowels to show present tense
This is a negative particle that means 'no' or 'not'
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Added alif with madda to show long vowel sound
Added و at beginning to connect this with what came before
This is the basic form of the personal pronoun meaning 'I'
Added short vowel marks (fatha) to show it means 'that' or 'indeed', not 'I'

steps.m: I (emphatic form used in this ayah to strongly emphasize 'I' am not worshipping)
steps.n: Added alif and small circle at the end to make it emphatic. In this ayah, it's emphasizing that the speaker is definitely not worshipping what others worship.
Connected the letters together with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed the pattern to show who does the action. This word describes the pronoun 'I' (أَنَا۠) in the verse.
Added the tanween mark to show this is indefinite (like saying 'a worshipper' instead of 'the worshipper')
Basic form of the word meaning 'what' - here it refers to whatever false gods were worshipped
Added shadda for emphasis and to connect smoothly with previous word
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adding تُ at the end changes it from 'he worshipped' to 'I worshipped'
Changed from 'I' to 'you all' by adding م at the end and doubling the ت
Added the fatha vowel mark on ل and lengthened the alif to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
The و at the start means 'and', connecting this phrase to what came before it. This word negates عٰبِدُونَ (worshippers) in the ayah.
This is the basic form of the pronoun used when talking to one male person
Adding مْ with ُ changes this from speaking to one person to speaking to many people (like changing 'you' to 'you all' in English)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: one who worships (describing أنتم - you all)
steps.n: Changed from 'worshipped' to 'worshipper' - like changing from 'ran' to 'runner'. The long aa sound (ٰ) and i sound (ِ) show it's describing who does the action.
The ون ending makes it plural for multiple worshippers
Basic form of the word that asks about or refers to things
Added fatha to show correct pronunciation
Extended the alif to show emphasis. In this ayah, it refers to what the speaker worships, connecting to أَعْبُدُ (I worship) that comes after it.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the past tense verb
Added أَ at beginning to show 'I' and changed vowels to show present tense
Added fatha to show the basic vowel of this preposition
Added the plural 'you' pronoun كُمْ to show who the preposition is directed to. This word connects to دِينُكُمْ (your religion) in the ayah.
Added kasra to first letter and connected letters to form the noun meaning religion
Added damma to show this is the subject
Added كُمْ to show the religion belongs to multiple people being addressed
Added fatha to show the vowel sound
Added لِ which shows direction or purpose

steps.m: and for me (connected to دِينِ - my religion)
steps.n: Added ىَ at the end to show this means 'me'. This word connects to دِينِ to mean 'my religion'

steps.m: to follow a way of life
steps.n: The meaning evolved from 'debt/obligation' to 'judgment/religion'
Root letters د-ي-ن became the noun دِين by adding kasra (small diagonal line) under د, meaning changed from 'following' to 'religion'
The kasra at the end shows this word is possessed by or connected to something else in the sentence
